description_long: This object is a robust axe featuring a long, slightly curved wooden handle with a natural grain pattern, designed for ergonomic grip and leverage. The head of the axe is made of metal, with a broad, flat striking face on one side and a wedge-shaped blade on the other. The blade is thick and heavy, suitable for splitting or breaking hard materials, and the poll is rounded and solid, providing balance and additional striking capability. The handle is securely fitted into the eye of the axe head, with no visible fasteners, suggesting a traditional wedged mounting. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with no decorative elements or branding visible. The materials and construction indicate it is built for durability and heavy-duty use in urban maintenance or demolition tasks.
description: A heavy-duty axe with a curved wooden handle and a thick, metal head featuring a wedge blade and flat poll. Built for strength and utility, it has a natural wood finish and a robust, functional design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden handle and the broad, flat face of the metal axe head.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular striking face and the way the handle is inserted into the head.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the wedge-shaped blade and the handle's curvature.
description_view_3: Top-down view focusing on the handle's grain and the attachment point to the axe head.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a natural grain, oval cross-section, ergonomic grip, and no additional grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Thick, wedge-shaped single blade with a broad, flat poll; no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the handle inserted through the eye of the axe head.
